title: Open Assistant Plugin Definition
description: Schema for defining a REST API plugin that is exposed as LLM tools.

id.description: Unique plugin identifier using lowercase letters, digits, and underscores. Used as a prefix for tool names.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI.

description.description: Short description of what this plugin integrates with.

icon.description: Emoji or short text icon displayed next to the plugin name.

base_url.description: Base URL for all API calls. Config field values can be substituted using {field_key} placeholders.

type.description: Authentication mechanism. 'bearer' → Authorization: Bearer {token}. 'header' → {header_name}: {token}. 'basic' → Authorization: Basic base64(username:password). 'api_key_with_jwt' → static {api_key_header} + JWT obtained via POST {token_endpoint}.

header_name.description: Required when type is 'header'. The HTTP header name to use.

fixed_password.description: Optional, for type 'basic'. When set, the password is hardcoded to this value and the user only provides a token/username. Example: Toggl uses 'api_token' as the fixed password.

api_key_header.description: For type 'api_key_with_jwt'. The HTTP header name for the static API key (e.g. 'X-apikey'). Defaults to 'X-API-Key'.

token_endpoint.description: For type 'api_key_with_jwt'. Path to POST username+password to in order to obtain a JWT (e.g. '/token'). Defaults to '/token'.

token_field.description: For type 'api_key_with_jwt'. JSON field name in the login response that contains the JWT (e.g. 'access_token'). Defaults to 'access_token'.

token_prefix.description: For type 'api_key_with_jwt'. Prefix for the Authorization header value (e.g. 'Access_Token' → 'Authorization: Access_Token <jwt>'). Defaults to 'Bearer'.

config_fields.description: Non-credential configuration fields (e.g. organization name, site URL). Stored in settings and substituted into URLs.

key.description: Field key used as a URL placeholder and settings key.

description.description: Help text shown below the input.

sensitive.description: If true, stored encrypted in service_credentials instead of settings.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown in the input field.

endpoints.description: List of API endpoints exposed as LLM tools. Each becomes a tool named plugin_{id}_{endpoint.name}.

name.description: Endpoint identifier (lowercase + underscores). Becomes the tool name suffix.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the Tools assignment screen.

description.description: Description shown to the LLM. Be specific about when to use this endpoint.

path.description: URL path appended to base_url. Supports {variable} placeholders for path parameters and config fields.

parameters.description: Parameters the LLM can pass when calling this tool.

in.description: Where to place the parameter: 'path' → substituted into URL, 'query' → appended as ?key=value, 'body' → JSON request body, 'header' → HTTP request header.

type.description: JSON Schema type for the parameter. Use 'array' for a list of primitives; pair it with an 'items' field describing the element type.

items.description: Required when type is 'array'. Describes the array element type. Nested arrays/objects are not supported.

type.description: Element type of the array. Must be a primitive.

default.description: Default value when the parameter is not provided.

in.description: Array parameters are only supported for in='query' or in='body'.
